name: Spaghetti Squash Salad
recipeInstructions:
1. Pierce the skin of the squash several times with a knife to allow steam to escape while cooking.
2. Place the whole squash in the microwave oven and cook on high for 15 minutes, turning once or twice. Remove from the oven or steamer and let stand 10 minutes. A steamer placed on top of the stove will also work well.
3. Alternatively, cut the squash in half lengthwise, remove the seeds, and place the cut side down in a pot with 2 inches of water. Cover and boil for 20 minutes.
4. In a small bowl, combine parsley, olive oil, margarine, and minced garlic. Cook on high for 1 minute (or heat in a pan on medium heat on your stove). Set aside.
5. Sauté mushrooms in the butter or margarine mixture.
6. Gently pull the spaghetti-like flesh away from the outer skin of the squash using a fork.
7. Combine the squash with the sautéed mushrooms.
8. Pour the butter mixture over the vegetables and sprinkle with cheese, salt, and pepper. Stir gently and serve.
Note: This yields about 1 serving per serving...which would make this ABOUT 1 VEGETABLE EXCHANGE + 1 FAT EXCHANGE (if you reduce the margarine, which I surely would!).
